actionable_feedback: Add a short cost-estimate column to the recommendation table showing per-action USD impact (or conservative ranges) and a summed total that demonstrates the remediation package fits within decision_budget_usd ($137,500).
Export or include a CSV or plain-text 'formula sheet' that lists the workbook formulas (or cell references) so verifiers without openpyxl can validate computations programmatically.

In owner_ticket.md and the workbook, explicitly state the formula for abs_residual_pct (e.g., abs(residual) / predicted_cost) and include the rounding policy used when comparing to sensitivity thresholds.

Annotate key workbook cells with references (sheet name and cell addresses) for slope, intercept, predicted_cost, residual, abs_residual_pct, and classification so human verifiers can quickly validate cell-level logic.
If possible, attach a screenshot or exported workbook showing formula cells, or ensure the repo includes a lightweight CSV+formula manifest as a fallback.

feedback_summary: The baseline attempt is high-quality and passes the rubric checks. Main limitations are (1) inability to programmatically validate spreadsheet formulas because openpyxl was unavailable in the execution environment, and (2) the owner remediation ticket lacks explicit per-action USD impact arithmetic or a short cost-estimate column demonstrating adherence to the supplied decision_budget_usd.
